title: Buying Kinesis Gold (KAU): Safest Exchanges and Steps
content:
Kinesis Gold (KAU) offers a way for crypto users to integrate physical gold into their portfolios without the logistical burdens of traditional commodity trading. By holding KAU, users gain exposure to real gold, which is stored in secure vaults and can be audited. As of April 29, 2026, the asset maintains a stable profile within the RWA sector, though potential buyers should be aware of its medium-risk status.

| Metric | Details |
| :--- | :--- |

## Why

Buy Kinesis Gold?
KAU allows users to earn a monthly yield paid in gold, a feature that distinguishes it from competitors like [[Tether](/tether) Gold](/tether-gold) or [PAX Gold](/pax-gold). Furthermore, the ability to spend gold via a virtual card makes it one of the most utility-forward gold tokens on the market today.

## Top Exchange Options
KAU can be purchased primarily through the official Kinesis platform, which provides direct access to the gold-backing mechanism and the fee-sharing yield program. Other decentralized exchanges may support KAU, but using the native Kinesis exchange is recommended to ensure seamless redemption capabilities.

## Purchase Tutorial
1. Register: Create an account on the official Kinesis Money platform.
2. Verification: Complete the mandatory KYC (Know Your Customer) process required for all RWA platforms.
3. Deposit: Fund your account using fiat or supported stablecoins.
4. Trade: Navigate to the market pair (e.g., USD/KAU) and execute your order at the desired price.
5. Yield: Once held in your Kinesis wallet, your balance will automatically qualify for the monthly fee-sharing payout.

## Securing Your Tokens
While you can store KAU on the Kinesis platform to earn yields, you may also move your tokens to a private, non-custodial wallet that supports the relevant token standard. However, holding KAU outside of the Kinesis platform may make it difficult to participate in the native yield-sharing program. Always use hardware wallets for any significant holdings.

## Important Risks
The TokenRadar Risk Score of 6 highlights that while KAU is a stable asset, it carries risks associated with centralized custody and external auditing. Always perform due diligence regarding the platform's solvency and the transparency of the physical vaults.

## FAQ
- Do I need a wallet to buy KAU? Yes, you need a digital wallet provided by the exchange to store your tokens.
- Is KYC mandatory? Yes, because KAU involves real-world commodities, users are required to verify their identity.
- Can I lose money? Like any asset, the value of KAU fluctuates with the spot price of gold, and there are risks associated with platform security and market liquidity.
